channel.title: GeoPulse. Geopolitics in brief
channel.description: Signal-first briefings on geopolitics, edited by Lavkesh Dwivedi.

item.title: U.S. seeks international help to reopen Strait of Hormuz as crude prices surge
item.description: US President Donald Trump will receive a briefing on plans for military strikes on Iran to make it more flexible on nuclear issues. U. S. President Donald Trump is slated to receive a briefing on April 30 on plans for a series of fresh military ‌strikes on Iran in hopes of making Iran more flexible on nuclear issues in negotiations.

item.title: Iran war has cost $25B, Pentagon says. And, SCOTUS strikes blow to Voting Rights Act
item.description: The Pentagon estimates the war with Iran has cost $25 billion so far. And, the Supreme Court ruled that Louisiana's 2024 election map was "an unconstitutional racial gerrymander.". Iran war has cost $25B, Pentagon says.

item.title: Zelensky demands Trump explain new ceasefire proposal after Putin’s call with US president
item.description: US President Trump had a "very good" phone call with Russian President Putin, who offered a ceasefire in Ukraine on Victory Day. The offer was welcomed by Trump. Ukraine-Russia war latest: Trump welcomes Putin’s offer of Victory Day ceasefire in ‘very good’ phone call The Independent.

item.title: Will the Iran war reshape the global energy order?
item.description: OPEC's grip on oil markets is under pressure as US exports rise and China drives the push towards renewables. As oil markets shift, OPEC's grip is under pressure, US exports rise, and China drives the push towards renewables.

item.title: Oil price hits highest since 2022 after report Trump to be briefed on new Iran options
item.description: Oil prices jumped after a report that US Central Command prepared a plan for strikes on Iran. Axios reported that US Central Command has prepared a plan for a wave of "short and powerful" strikes on Iran.

item.title: Indian-origin woman, among 3 activists, fined for staging pro-Palestine protest in Singapore
item.description: An Indian-origin woman was fined for staging a pro-Palestine protest in Singapore. Court emphasises the responsibility of organisers to be aware of restricted areas, amidst growing tensions surrounding the Israel-Palestine conflict.

item.title: West Asia War sends Pakistan’s weekly oil import bill spiralling to 167% to $800 million
item.description: Pakistan's weekly oil import bill increased by 167% to $800 million due to the West Asia War. West Asia War sends Pakistan’s weekly oil import bill spiralling to 167% to $800 million.

item.title: Kyiv shuns Moscow's 'Victory Day truce' asking for long-term ceasefire
item.description: Kyiv rejected Moscow's proposed Victory Day truce, instead seeking a long-term ceasefire. Ukrainian officials cited ongoing Russian aggression as a reason for their stance. Kyiv shuns Moscow's 'Victory Day truce' asking for long-term ceasefire Euronews. com.

item.title: Mexico’s cartel crackdown hits top ranks – but will it fuel Jalisco violence?
item.description: Mexico's military arrested a top Jalisco New Generation cartel leader hiding in a drainage pipe, dealing a significant blow to the organisation. The move highlights renewed tactics and fears of cartel infighting.

item.title: 'Immense irony' as Iran war fuels 'boom' in renewable power
item.description: The Iran war has unexpectedly driven a surge in renewable energy adoption, according to the UN climate chief. This "immense irony" has raised hopes for a global shift away from fossil fuels.

item.title: Colombia climate talks end with hopes raised for fossil fuel phaseout
item.description: Nearly 60 countries agreed to develop national roadmaps to phase out fossil fuels at a landmark climate meeting in Colombia. The move aims to wean the world off coal, oil, and gas.

item.title: Opinion | Pak Is Finally Back In Middle East's 'Good Books'. But Can It Stay There?
item.description: Pakistan has regained its position as a key player in the Middle East, but its nuclear status makes this a challenging and delicate space to maintain. This is both an enviable and challenging space to be in for the only Islamic nation in the world that has nuclear weapons.

item.title: Who did Israel hit on Lebanon’s ‘Black Wednesday’?
item.description: Israel targeted Hezbollah in Lebanon, but experts question the evidence. Israel says it targeted Hezbollah, but experts say the evidence proves otherwise. Who did Israel hit on Lebanon’s ‘Black Wednesday’?

item.title: Rise in caesarean section births in Gaza brings danger and infection risks
item.description: Gaza's rising caesarean section births pose danger and infection risks to mothers. Caesarean Awareness Month is marked in April. Conditions in Gaza can lead to risks for mothers who undergo the procedure.

item.title: Trump Has 3 "Military Options" Against Iran Amid Stalemate. What They Are
item.description: Trump considers three military options against Iran amid a stalemate. Tthe Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staf is also expected to attend CENTCOM's meeting with Trump. Trump Has 3 "Military Options" Against Iran Amid Stalemate.

item.title: US Supreme Court scraps Louisiana’s Black-majority district in major blow to voting rights
item.description: The US Supreme Court scrapped Louisiana's Black-majority district, affecting voting rights. US Supreme Court scraps Louisiana’s Black-majority district in major blow to voting rights.

item.title: Why Iran's Oil Industry Is Being Squeezed By US Blockade
item.description: The US blockade squeezes Iran's oil industry, affecting tankers like the Asahi Princess. The Greek-flagged crude oil tanker "Asahi Princess". Why Iran's Oil Industry Is Being Squeezed By US Blockade.

item.title: Environmental disaster fears grip Russia as Ukraine targets oil refineries
item.description: Ukrainian forces hit Russia's Tuapse oil refinery, causing spills and black rain. Ukrainian forces have hammered one of Russia's largest sites in Tuapse, causing oil spills and black rain. Environmental disaster fears grip Russia as Ukraine targets oil refineries.

item.title: Ukraine Bets On Battlefield AI As The Race For Weapons Autonomy Intensifies
item.description: Ukraine has over 2,000 domestic arms manufacturers and military technology firms. Ukraine's domestic arms sector includes more than 2,000 manufacturers and military technology firms. Ukraine Bets On Battlefield AI As The Race For Weapons Autonomy Intensifies.

item.title: Hezbollah Adopts A New Weapon Against Israel: Fiber-Optic Drones
item.description: Hezbollah uses fiber-optic drones that are not controlled remotely. But fiber-optic drones are not controlled remotely. (Representational Image). Hezbollah Adopts A New Weapon Against Israel: Fiber-Optic Drones.
